mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window or negative mean return when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy elevates corporate borrowing costs and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5.0 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in consumer prices and a federal funds rate above 5% indicate a stagflationary impulse where tighter policy fails to contain inflation, eroding real earnings and generating negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS yields a t-statistic below 1.5 in a pooled regression predicting 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as corporate revenue growth stalls during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters when GDPC1 declines during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production signals stronger manufacturing activity and inventory investment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward return following INDPRO increases is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over three months, household income expectations deteriorate and consumer discretionary spending contracts, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 45% of triggered instances over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in retail sales and nonfarm payrolls reinforce a broad demand expansion narrative, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days that exceed the signal from either series in isolation.
failure_condition: The joint signal of RSAFS and PAYEMS increases does not outperform the single-series PAYEMS signal by at least 2 percentage points annualized over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: During restrictive monetary regimes, a month-over-month increase in the consumer price index forces the market to reprice terminal rate expectations higher, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day returns following CPIAUCSL increases are positive in more than 50% of observations during restrictive policy periods.
